category_title: Students with mild intellectual disability and leadership potential
student_description: Ben is a student with mild intellectual disability who possesses strong logical thinking and leadership potential, but struggles with low motivation and nervousness during the initial phases of self-regulated learning. He requires specific role assignments that leverage his strengths and relies heavily on direct, face-to-face positive evaluation, as he tends to ignore asynchronous digital feedback.

criteria.rationale: Ben needs dedicated, recurring 1-on-1 time to reflect on his learning strategies and expand his goal-setting capabilities to build self-regulation over time.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes regular (e.g., termly) individual interviews focused on discussing learning strategies, self-evaluation, and goal setting.

criteria.rationale: Because Ben takes time to master self-regulated learning strategies, he requires consistent, intensive practice of the same skills across different contexts.
criteria.check: The long-term plan coordinates the teaching and reinforcement of specific self-regulated learning skills across multiple subjects simultaneously.
